export_requirements.export_permit: No formal export permit. USDA endorsement of health certificate serves as export documentation.

import_requirements.microchip: Required (ISO 11784/11785). Must be implanted before rabies vaccination
import_requirements.rabies_vaccination: Required. Minimum age: 12 weeks. Vaccination window is strict: minimum 30 days before arrival, maximum 12 months.
import_requirements.titre_test: Required for: Pets from Group 2 and Group 3 countries. Not required for: Pets from Group 1 countries (rabies-free: UK, Australia, Japan, NZ, etc.). Minimum: 0.5 IU/ml
import_requirements.import_permit: Required. Must be obtained BEFORE travel. Permit specifies conditions and quarantine requirements.
import_requirements.health_certificate: Required. Official veterinary health certificate. Issued by: Government veterinarian in country of export. Certificate must confirm: vaccination status, microchip number, health status, absence of infectious disease

airlines.name: Emirates
airlines.policy_summary: Cargo: Emirates SkyCargo handles all pet transport as manifested cargo. Snub-nosed (brachycephalic) breeds accepted with additional requirements and seasonal restrictions. Temperature embargoes during extreme heat (summer months in Gulf region)

airlines.name: Virgin Atlantic
airlines.policy_summary: Cargo: Pets transported through Virgin Atlantic Cargo. Brachycephalic breeds restricted. Temperature embargoes

timeline_steps.timing: Before any vaccinations. Must be done first.
At least 21 days before travel (after microchip)
Blood drawn 30+ days after vaccination. 90-day wait after successful result.

timeline_steps.action: Apply for import permit from Hong Kong

timeline_steps.timing: Within 10 days of travel (check destination-specific requirements)

timeline_steps.action: Government endorsement of health certificate (United States)
timeline_steps.timing: After health certificate issued, before travel

timeline_steps.action: Book IATA-compliant flight and arrange pet cargo/cabin booking
timeline_steps.timing: 2-4 weeks before travel. Confirm pet space availability.

timeline_steps.action: Travel day: arrive early, present all documentation
timeline_steps.timing: 3-4 hours before departure for cargo pets

cost_factors: Airline cargo/cabin fees (varies by carrier, pet weight, and crate size)
Pet transport agent fee (if using a relocation service)
key_warnings: Brachycephalic (flat-faced) breeds face cargo restrictions on most airlines. Check breed-specific policies before booking.
Microchip MUST be implanted before rabies vaccination. Vaccination given before microchip is invalid and the clock restarts.

seo.title: Pet Transport from United States to Hong Kong | Complete Guide
seo.description: How to transport your dog or cat from United States to Hong Kong. Import requirements, airline options, costs, and step-by-step process.

content.h1: Pet Transport from United States to Hong Kong
content.overview:
Shipping a cat between United States and Hong Kong is one of the moderately complex international pet transport routes.

Expect to start preparations 6-10 weeks before your travel date. A rabies titre test (blood test) is required, which means an additional waiting period after vaccination. You'll need to apply for an import permit from Hong Kong before your pet can travel. Airlines serving this route include Emirates, British Airways, and Virgin Atlantic.

This guide covers everything: Hong Kong's import rules, what you need from United States's side, airlines, costs, and timelines.

sections.heading: Key things to know about this route
sections.body: Compared to some international routes, United States to Hong Kong is relatively straightforward. That doesn't mean you can leave it to the last minute.

sections.heading: Practical tips for shipping your pet from United States to Hong Kong
sections.body:
Start the microchip and vaccination process as early as possible. The biggest mistake people make is leaving it too late. Once you miss a deadline, the entire timeline pushes back.

All airlines on this route transport pets as cargo, not in the cabin. That means your pet flies in a temperature-controlled hold in an IATA-approved crate. It's standard practice and well-regulated.

Use a pet transport agent if you're not confident handling the paperwork yourself. A good agent knows the current rules, has relationships with airline cargo departments, and can troubleshoot problems before they become disasters.

sections.heading: The day your pet flies
sections.body:
Arrive at the airport at least 3 hours before departure if your pet is travelling as cargo. You'll need to present all original documents: health certificate, vaccination records, microchip number, and any import permits.

Your pet's crate must be clean, labelled with your contact details and the consignee's details at the destination, and have food and water containers attached. Live animal stickers and orientation arrows are required.

On arrival in Hong Kong, you (or your agent) collect your pet from the cargo terminal. There may be an inspection by veterinary authorities at the airport. Have copies of all paperwork ready.

faqs.question: Does Hong Kong require quarantine for pets from United States?
faqs.answer: It depends on your specific circumstances. Check requirements. Check with the destination authorities or a pet transport specialist for your exact situation.

faqs.question: Which airlines transport pets from United States to Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: Airlines operating pet cargo on this route include Emirates, British Airways, Virgin Atlantic. All transport pets as manifested cargo in temperature-controlled holds, not in the cabin. You'll need an IATA-compliant crate and should book cargo space well in advance. Some airlines have seasonal restrictions and brachycephalic breed embargoes.

faqs.question: How far in advance should I start preparing to move my pet from United States to Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: Start at least 6-10 weeks before your planned travel date. The process begins with microchipping, then rabies vaccination (with a mandatory waiting period), then any required blood tests, health certificates, and permit applications. Rushing the timeline is the most common reason people hit problems.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to transport a dog from United States to Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: The total cost depends on your dog's size, the airline, and whether you use a transport agent. For a medium-sized dog, expect to pay for: veterinary fees (vaccinations, health certificate, microchip), an IATA-compliant crate, airline cargo fees, government endorsements, and any import permits or quarantine fees at the destination. Using a door-to-door agent adds to the cost but handles the complexity for you.

faqs.question: What documents does my pet need to travel from United States to Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: Your pet will need: proof of ISO microchip, rabies vaccination certificate, rabies titre test results, official health certificate, import permit. The health certificate must be issued by an official veterinarian within 10 days of travel. Some documents need government endorsement. Check every requirement against the current rules, because regulations change and an outdated document can mean your pet doesn't fly.

faqs.question: Can I fly my French Bulldog or Pug from United States to Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: Brachycephalic (flat-faced) breeds face restrictions on most airlines, particularly for cargo transport. These breeds are more vulnerable to breathing difficulties at altitude and in heat. Many airlines impose seasonal embargoes (typically summer months), and some restrict flat-faced breeds from cargo year-round. A few airlines still accept them with additional veterinary clearance. Check the specific airline's current policy before booking.
